title: trifolium longipes f. atrorubens
intro: Trifolium longipes f. atrorubens, also known as the Alpine Clover, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help in treating wounds, skin irritations, and other infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help protect against cell damage, boost the immune system, and prevent certain diseases. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
